treatments.name: Chlorothalonil 75% WP
treatments.application: Foliar spray every 7-10 days starting at first symptom

treatments.name: Mancozeb 75% WP
treatments.application: Preventive spray every 10 days, start before symptom onset

treatments.name: Azoxystrobin 23% SC
treatments.application: Spray at early infection, repeat after 14 days

treatments.name: Copper Oxychloride 50% WP
treatments.application: Spray every 7-14 days as preventive

treatments.name: Trichoderma viride
treatments.dosage: 5g per liter of water (soil application: 1kg per acre mixed with FYM)
treatments.application: Soil application before planting + foliar spray at 30-day intervals

treatments.name: Crop Rotation + Resistant Varieties
treatments.application: Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2-3 years. Use resistant varieties like Arka Rakshak, Arka Samrat

treatments.name: Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP
treatments.application: Spray immediately on detection; repeat every 7 days in wet weather

treatments.name: Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP
treatments.application: Preventive + curative; spray at 7-10 day intervals

treatments.name: Dimethomorph 50% WP
treatments.application: Foliar spray at early infection stage

treatments.name: Bordeaux Mixture (1%)
treatments.application: Spray as protective coating before rain season starts

treatments.name: Dinocap 48% EC
treatments.application: Spray at first symptom; repeat every 10-15 days

treatments.name: Hexaconazole 5% SC
treatments.application: Spray at disease onset

treatments.name: Sulphur 80% WP (Wettable Sulphur)
treatments.application: Spray every 10-15 days; avoid in temperatures above 35°C

treatments.name: Neem Oil 3%
treatments.dosage: 5ml per liter of water + liquid soap
treatments.application: Spray weekly as preventive

treatments.name: Streptocycline (Streptomycin Sulphate 90% + Tetracycline 10%)
treatments.application: Spray at first symptom; repeat after 10 days

treatments.name: Copper Hydroxide 77% WP
treatments.application: Preventive spray before monsoon season

treatments.name: Resistant Varieties + Water Management
treatments.application: Use BLB-resistant varieties (Improved Samba Mahsuri, Rajendra Dhan). Ensure proper drainage; avoid excess nitrogen (>120 kg/ha)

treatments.name: Tricyclazole 75% WP
treatments.application: Spray at tillering + boot leaf + panicle emergence stages

treatments.name: Isoprothiolane 40% EC
treatments.application: Spray 2-3 times at 15-day intervals during vegetative stage

treatments.name: Pseudomonas fluorescens
treatments.dosage: 10g per liter of water (seed treatment: 10g per kg seed)
treatments.application: Seed treatment + 3 foliar sprays at 15-day intervals starting 20 DAT

treatments.name: Metalaxyl 35% WS
treatments.application: Seed treatment before sowing (pearl millet, sunflower)

treatments.name: Fosetyl-Al 80% WP
treatments.application: Foliar spray at first symptom; systemic action

treatments.name: Trichoderma harzianum
treatments.dosage: 2kg per acre mixed with well-decomposed FYM (200kg)
treatments.application: Apply to soil at planting; no curative chemical treatment exists

treatments.name: Soil Solarization + Resistant Varieties
treatments.dosage: Cover soil with transparent polythene for 45 days in summer
treatments.application: Solarize soil before planting in heavily infested fields. Use resistant rootstocks (tomato: Arka Rakshak; banana: Grand Naine AAA genome)

treatments.name: Carbendazim 50% WP (Soil Drench)
treatments.dosage: 2g per liter of water; 200ml per plant (drench around root zone)
treatments.application: Soil drench at first symptom of wilting; slows disease but does not cure

treatments.name: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL
treatments.application: Spray at 15 and 30 DAS to control whitefly vector
treatments.effectiveness: High (prevents spread, no cure for infected plants)

treatments.name: Thiamethoxam 25% WG
treatments.application: Seed treatment (3g/kg seed) + foliar spray at peak whitefly activity

treatments.name: Resistant Varieties + Early Sowing
treatments.application: Use resistant varieties (Mung: SML 668, Samrat; Soybean: JS 335, JS 9560). Sow early to avoid peak whitefly season. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately

treatments.name: Carbendazim 50% WP
treatments.application: Spray at flowering + fruit development stage

treatments.name: Propineb 70% WP
treatments.application: Alternating spray with Carbendazim every 10 days during fruiting

treatments.name: Hot Water Treatment (for fruits)
treatments.application: Dip harvested mangoes/papayas before storage to prevent post-harvest anthracnose

treatments.name: Cartap Hydrochloride 4G (granules)
treatments.dosage: 8-10 kg per acre broadcast in standing water
treatments.application: Apply granules in flooded paddy at 30 DAT (when dead hearts appear)

treatments.name: Trichogramma japonicum (egg parasitoid)
treatments.dosage: 8-10 Tricho-cards per acre (approx. 1.5 lakh parasitized eggs)
treatments.application: Release at 28 and 35 DAT, coinciding with moth egg-laying period

treatments.name: Light Trap + Clipping Tips
treatments.application: Install light traps to attract moths. Clip seedling tips before transplanting to destroy eggs

treatments.name: Spiromesifen 22.9% SC
treatments.application: Spray at 20 and 40 DAT to kill whitefly nymphs

treatments.name: Tolerant Varieties + Border Crops
treatments.application: Use ToLCV-tolerant varieties (Arka Rakshak, Arka Ananya). Plant maize/sorghum border rows as windbreaks to reduce whitefly entry. Use 40-mesh nylon net nurseries

treatments.name: Propiconazole 25% EC
treatments.application: Spray at first sign of pustules; critical at flag leaf stage

treatments.name: Tebuconazole 25.9% EC
treatments.application: Single spray at heading stage provides 3-4 weeks protection

treatments.name: Resistant Varieties
treatments.application: Grow multi-rust-resistant varieties: HD 3086, DBW 187, WH 1270. Timely sowing (Nov 1-15) to escape late rust buildup
